news.title:
JK Cement Ltd acquires a 9.77% stake in Mehrauni Electro Power Pvt Ltd for Rs. 2.1 crore.
MEPPL focuses on renewable energy, including a 40 MW solar plant in Uttar Pradesh.
news.summary:
JK Cement Ltd acquires a 9.77% stake in Mehrauni Electro Power Pvt Ltd for Rs. 2.1 crore.
MEPPL focuses on renewable energy, including a 40 MW solar plant in Uttar Pradesh.

news.title:
Jefferies cuts JK Cement target price to ₹6,705 from ₹7,000.
Energy cost surge may raise costs by ₹300/t; EBITDA cut by 4–9% FY27–28.
news.summary:
Jefferies cuts JK Cement target price to ₹6,705 from ₹7,000.
Energy cost surge may raise costs by ₹300/t; EBITDA cut by 4–9% FY27–28.

news.title:
Top cement firms to report strong Q4 FY26 volumes driven by construction and govt spending.
Profitability could be impacted due to rising fuel and packaging costs, say analysts.
news.summary:
Top cement firms to report strong Q4 FY26 volumes driven by construction and govt spending.
Profitability could be impacted due to rising fuel and packaging costs, say analysts.

news.title:
HDFC Securities lists top cement stocks like Ambuja, UltraTech, JK Cement amid resilient demand but weak pricing power.
Energy costs may rise Rs 300-400/MT FY27-28, impacting margins; Ambuja Cement's target multiple cut to 15.5x.
news.summary:
HDFC Securities lists top cement stocks like Ambuja, UltraTech, JK Cement amid resilient demand but weak pricing power.
Energy costs may rise Rs 300-400/MT FY27-28, impacting margins; Ambuja Cement's target multiple cut to 15.5x.

news.title:
India's cement sector to grow 7-8% in FY26, led by large players like UltraTech Cement and Ambuja Cements at 12% and 10% growth respectively.
JK Cement may see 14% growth; cement prices up 2-3% QoQ, offsetting input costs. Diesel price hike post-elections could pressure margins.
news.summary:
India's cement sector to grow 7-8% in FY26, led by large players like UltraTech Cement and Ambuja Cements at 12% and 10% growth respectively.
JK Cement may see 14% growth; cement prices up 2-3% QoQ, offsetting input costs. Diesel price hike post-elections could pressure margins.

news.title:
JK Cement issued a certificate for Q4 FY26 CP fund usage; Rs. 225 Cr redeemed.
CP proceeds used for working capital; no adverse financial changes observed.
news.summary:
JK Cement issued a certificate for Q4 FY26 CP fund usage; Rs. 225 Cr redeemed.
CP proceeds used for working capital; no adverse financial changes observed.

news.title:
Cement stocks, including Nuvoco, JK Cement, UltraTech, Dalmia Bharat, and Ambuja, fell 1-3% despite HSBC's positive outlook.
HSBC set price targets, with Nuvoco at ₹420 (+38.5%), Ambuja at ₹590 (+39.9%), and UltraTech at ₹13,750 (+22.7%).
news.summary:
Cement stocks, including Nuvoco, JK Cement, UltraTech, Dalmia Bharat, and Ambuja, fell 1-3% despite HSBC's positive outlook.
HSBC set price targets, with Nuvoco at ₹420 (+38.5%), Ambuja at ₹590 (+39.9%), and UltraTech at ₹13,750 (+22.7%).

news.title:
HSBC initiates coverage on JK Cement with Hold rating, target Rs 5740.
FY27 capacity peaks, FY28 supply-demand improves; Apr-May price hikes to offset energy costs.
news.summary:
HSBC initiates coverage on JK Cement with Hold rating, target Rs 5740.
FY27 capacity peaks, FY28 supply-demand improves; Apr-May price hikes to offset energy costs.

news.title:
J.K. Cement saw an NSE block trade of ~75,828 shares at Rs. 4981.
The trade amounted to Rs. 37.77 crores, marking significant activity.
news.summary:
J.K. Cement saw an NSE block trade of ~75,828 shares at Rs. 4981.
The trade amounted to Rs. 37.77 crores, marking significant activity.

news.title:
JK Cement increased Muddapur grinding capacity from 3.5 to 4.5 MnTPA.
Grey cement capacity now totals 32.26 MnTPA; expansion achieved via debottlenecking.
news.summary:
JK Cement increased Muddapur grinding capacity from 3.5 to 4.5 MnTPA.
Grey cement capacity now totals 32.26 MnTPA; expansion achieved via debottlenecking.

news.title:
JK Cement wins bid for 350-hectare limestone block in MP, aiding raw material security.
Q3FY25 net profit fell 23% YoY to ₹57 crore; shares down 1.8% at ₹5,016, 6-month decline 26.9%.
news.summary:
JK Cement wins bid for 350-hectare limestone block in MP, aiding raw material security.
Q3FY25 net profit fell 23% YoY to ₹57 crore; shares down 1.8% at ₹5,016, 6-month decline 26.9%.

news.title:
JK Cement declared preferred bidder for Itauri Jharkua limestone block in MP.
The mining lease covers 350 hectares; e-auction held by MP Govt.
news.summary:
JK Cement declared preferred bidder for Itauri Jharkua limestone block in MP.
The mining lease covers 350 hectares; e-auction held by MP Govt.

news.title: JK Cement received a notice from J&K Legal Metrology Department to stop weight-based packaging.
news.summary: JK Cement received a notice from J&K Legal Metrology Department to stop weight-based packaging.

news.title:
JK Cement's unit, Saifco, received a notice under the Legal Metrology Act for packaging violations.
No material financial impact reported; company has sought an extension to respond to the notice.
news.summary:
JK Cement's unit, Saifco, received a notice under the Legal Metrology Act for packaging violations.
No material financial impact reported; company has sought an extension to respond to the notice.

news.title:
JK Cement faces Rs 250/ton cost pressure from rising fuel, raw material prices.
The company expects modest price hikes and 7-8% demand growth in Q4 YoY.
news.summary:
JK Cement faces Rs 250/ton cost pressure from rising fuel, raw material prices.
The company expects modest price hikes and 7-8% demand growth in Q4 YoY.

news.title:
Cement stocks fell up to 7% due to margin concerns from rising crude oil prices.
Ramco dropped 7% to ₹1,014.6; JK 6% to ₹5,100; UltraTech 5% to ₹11,350.4; India Cements 5% to ₹362.9.
news.summary:
Cement stocks fell up to 7% due to margin concerns from rising crude oil prices.
Ramco dropped 7% to ₹1,014.6; JK 6% to ₹5,100; UltraTech 5% to ₹11,350.4; India Cements 5% to ₹362.9.

news.title:
Bihar CM inaugurated Coca Cola bottling plant & JK Cement unit worth ₹1,700 cr.
Projects aim at industrial development and creating 10M jobs in Bihar.
news.summary:
Bihar CM inaugurated Coca Cola bottling plant & JK Cement unit worth ₹1,700 cr.
Projects aim at industrial development and creating 10M jobs in Bihar.

news.title:
Top cement firms saw profit growth in Q3 FY26 despite price pressures.
Volume gains and operating leverage offset GST rate impact and lack of price hikes.
news.summary:
Top cement firms saw profit growth in Q3 FY26 despite price pressures.
Volume gains and operating leverage offset GST rate impact and lack of price hikes.

news.title:
JK Cement Ltd commissioned a 3 MnTPA grinding unit in Buxar, Bihar.
This is part of its 6 MnTPA expansion, raising total capacity to 31.26 MnTPA.
news.summary:
JK Cement Ltd commissioned a 3 MnTPA grinding unit in Buxar, Bihar.
This is part of its 6 MnTPA expansion, raising total capacity to 31.26 MnTPA.

news.title:
JK Cement executed an NSE block trade for 33,388 shares at Rs. 5670 each.
The transaction totaled Rs. 18.93 crore, highlighting significant market activity.
news.summary:
JK Cement executed an NSE block trade for 33,388 shares at Rs. 5670 each.
The transaction totaled Rs. 18.93 crore, highlighting significant market activity.
